balls.commentary: some nice inswing first ball. Shami lands it on Head's pad, as the batter looks to clip but gets it off his pad. Pant dives to his right to save a few runs
shorter side of a length, and just outside leg. Head opens his stance up to nudge to square leg
amazing bowling. Shami not only finds sharp inswing but also lands it as a yorker. The ball arrives bang on the base of leg stump, and Head swings to square leg
off the mark, but off the inside edge. Head looks to drive at this length ball angling across and pitching on off, but the ball dribbles to cover
another near yorker. He ties Abhishek down with the inswing, and goes on leg. Pushed down to mid-on

gone!! The slip was just removed, but the man at short third completes a nice take. Siddharth placed there went low and dived to his right to grab the ball just above the ground. It was an outside edge off Abhishek's bat, as he went driving at this full delivery outside off. It was a slower ball, th
balls.dismissal: Abhishek Sharma c Siddharth b Mohammed Shami 0 (2b 0x4 0x6 4m) SR: 0

balls.commentary: pulled to fine leg. Digvesh starts with a short ball just outside leg, and Head pulls after clearing his front leg
short again, and on leg. This one turns away, and is much quicker at 101kph. Head punches it down to mid-on's right, as Prince stops the ball with a slide
short of a length on leg, and finishing on the stumps. Kishan blocks it back to Digvesh
on the bounce to the man at cover point. Short in length, and wide of off, as Kishan slashes but doesn't find the gap
fuller in length, and again bowls faster. Driven from just outside off to long-off
on a length, as it lands around leg and turns away. Punched to long-off from middle

great catching!! LSG's fielding and Shami's skills have combined for the second wicket of the day. Head is beaten by the lack of pace as well - like Abhishek was. Shami bowls it on a short of a length on off, as he angles it across, and bowls with a scrambled seam at just 121kph. Head looks to push
balls.dismissal: Travis Head c Markram b Mohammed Shami 7 (8b 1x4 0x6 11m) SR: 87.5

balls.commentary: averted by the man coming into SRH's side today. Short of a length, and around a sixth-stump line. Livingstone lets it go
shorter side of a length, and just outside off. But the ball shapes away, and Livingstone again leaves it
full ball swinging in, and landing outside leg. He looks to flick but misses. Wide called. The first bad ball from Shami today
now Shami bowls it fuller. The length is fullish on middle, and Livingstone dabs it down
another dot ball - and almost a single. The man at short extra cover fumbles, but the ball dribbles to mid-off. Livingstone drove it forward off a fullish length around off, but not in the gap
dabbed late to deep third, as Livingstone shifts his weight back to this short-of-a-length ball around fifth stump

balls.description: Prince Yadav to Livingstone, no run
balls.commentary: short in length, and around sixth stump. Punched powerfully to point

good running, although he made it in time. Shorter side of a length on off, and punched to mid-on after the ball seamed into him. Digvesh at mid-on did well to rush forward and aim for the stumps at his end

bowled him!! Prince celebrates like a royal. He has flattened Kishan's off stump. Prince angled it across, and went with an upright seam. The ball landed around off or thereabouts, and Kishan went driving. But the ball just about shaped back in enough to smack the off stump, as Kishan was beaten
balls.dismissal: Ishan Kishan b Prince Yadav 1 (4b 0x4 0x6 13m) SR: 25

balls.description: Prince Yadav to Klaasen, no run
balls.commentary: good start. Shorter side of a length, and just outside off. Klaasen looks to block but is beaten on the outside edge as the ball straightens after pitching

balls.description: Prince Yadav to Klaasen, no run
balls.commentary: full in length, but the line in the channel outside off remains the same. Klaasen lets it go

balls.description: Prince Yadav to Klaasen, no run
balls.commentary: shorter side of a length on off, and punched towards mid-off
on the bounce to the man at square leg. Shami bowls it on a length on middle, and Livingstone whips it away

another slower ball, as the SRH batters can barely find a solution to it. Shami bowls this on the shorter side of a length, and around fifth stump. Livingstone is beaten on the inside edge as he looks to fend away from his body
a leading edge, and the ball goes over cover point. He went flicking at this length delivery seaming into his pads, but seemed to have played early
short of a length on off, and Livingstone is hit on his back thigh as he looks to pull but misses. The ball would have gone over the stumps
this time the slower ball is sliced, although not very convincingly. Shami bowls full and around sixth stump, as Livingstone mistimes an attempted loft over wide mid-off. Two more for him
he decides to counter Shami by walking down, but only ends up dabbing at this shortish ball around sixth stump to short third. Another great over from Shami
full and quick, and angling into middle and leg. Clipped to square leg
again bowls full, but this is on middle. Punched to cover
darts this into a stump-to-stump line, and bowls on a length. Punched to long-off by Klaasen
full and quick, and on off or thereabouts. Blocked down to cover
bang!! The first six of the day. Livingstone skips down the pitch, and clobbers this length ball on off over long-on. It's a liberal swing of the arms
much fuller, and angled into off. Driven back to Siddharth

balls.description: Avesh Khan to Klaasen, no run
balls.commentary: starts with a short-of-a-length ball just outside off, and Klaasen punches to point
bowls short, and just outside off. Pulled to deep square leg, as the man isn't all the way back at the boundary
goes full, and around the sixth-stump channel. Livingstone taps to deep third by playing late, and with bat behind pad
lack of pace, and short and wide outside off. Klaasen rides the bounce to swat it to deep cover point

balls.description: Avesh Khan to Livingstone, no run
balls.commentary: short of a length, and slightly wider outside off. Punched to point as he plays late again
slower ball again, and another short ball as well. This pitches in the corridor just outside off, and is pulled to deep square leg by rolling his wrists

superb take by Pant!! He first leaped in excitement, then moved to his left with the angle, and finally dived across to his right to gobble it one-handed. This is what happened with the ball: Livingstone went for the lap sweep, but ended up top-edging it. The ball popped up behind him, and Pant was
balls.dismissal: Liam Livingstone c †Pant b Rathi 14 (20b 0x4 1x6 24m) SR: 70

balls.description: Rathi to Nitish Kumar, no run
balls.commentary: shortish in length, and cut away to point from wide of off
another short ball from Digvesh. He bowls this wide of off, and the ball turns away. Cut away to deep point
Digvesh keeps going short. This is wide of off again, and cut away after the turn, for a single to deep cover point

balls.description: Rathi to Nitish Kumar, no run
balls.commentary: "uuuuiii," says Pant!! On a length, and around fifth stump, as the ball turns away slightly to go past the outside edge as Nitish looked to have a jab at it
full and flatter, and just outside off. Driven to deep cover point

balls.description: Shami to Nitish Kumar, no run
balls.commentary: beaten. Nitish leaps and looks to ramp this short delivery around sixth stump, but misses. Shami bowled that at 139kph

balls.description: Shami to Nitish Kumar, no run
balls.commentary: follows a short ball with a yorker. This is launched at off stump, and Nitish jams his bat down to drive it back to Shami, who stops it with his right boot

balls.description: Shami to Nitish Kumar, no run
balls.commentary: shorter side of a length on off, and nudged to the man close in at midwicket
lack of pace from Shami this time. He bowls on a length, and around sixth stump. Slapped to deep cover point
back to the short ball. This is slightly wide of off, and Klaasen swat-pulls to long-on

balls.description: Shami to Nitish Kumar, no run
balls.commentary: nope, but he finishes off a very impactful spell. It's 2 for 9 after four overs. This is a fullish delivery just outside off, and Nitish drives to the man at short cover
bottom edge, and past Pant. Klaasen goes slashing at this length ball slightly wide of off, but doesn't get the timing and placement right. The ball goes between Pant's legs for three

balls.description: Siddharth to Nitish Kumar, no run
balls.commentary: fuller and quicker, and on off. Punched to short cover

balls.description: Siddharth to Nitish Kumar, no run
balls.commentary: loud appeal for lbw, but turned down. I thought that was heading down the leg side. Siddharth asks Pant what he thinks, and the LSG captain doesn't review. Nitish went for the reverse sweep at this length ball angled into leg, and pitching there. But the angle would have taken it down further

balls.description: Siddharth to Nitish Kumar, no run
balls.commentary: much fuller, and quicker. On leg, and he drives to short midwicket
this time drives to long-off. He plays hard at this full ball on middle and off
Wow. He stands outside leg after giving himself room, and Siddharth goes on a length wide of off. Left alone by Klaasen

balls.description: Prince Yadav to Nitish Kumar,  SIX runs
balls.commentary: Second six of the innings, SRH have starved for it. They get it with a wide fullish ball that Reddy slashes off the front foot over long-off. Mostly a diagonal bat

balls.description: Prince Yadav to Nitish Kumar,  1 run
balls.commentary: Back of length, angling in, flicked off the back foot to midwicket
Low full toss on off, flicked to deep-backward square

balls.description: Prince Yadav to Nitish Kumar,  1 run
balls.commentary: Short just outside off, Reddy stays still and pulls early. Finds long-on

balls.description: Prince Yadav to Klaasen,  SIX runs
balls.commentary: Full toss has been smacked with disdain. Thigh-high, angling in on off stump, Klaasen waits for it and swings across the line to clear the midwicket fence. 78 metres six
Seemed slow. Yeah, it is. Dug in very short, spins in towards off stump. Klaasen accounts for it and times the pull nicely, to deep midwicket
A bit of room and a touch short, cut to deep point. Klaasen doesn't need room to cut

balls.description: Rathi to Nitish Kumar, FOUR runs
balls.commentary: Cuffed down the ground, beats long-on to the left. Back of length stock ball, may have turned away a touch, Reddy clears the front foot and swivels to pull it into the ground

balls.description: Rathi to Nitish Kumar, FOUR runs
balls.commentary: Cut very late, effectively too. Beats backward point. Nitish ready for the change in line, knows Rathi won't bowl too full, so he slashes when the ball almost passed his bat
Tight two, they make it. Ball never crossed the ring. Nitish flicked the fullish ball and took off right away. No one in the ring there, long-on had to run in
Length outside off, punched off the back foot through cover
Vertical-bat pull almost, Klaasen winding up big and bringing the bat down with force. Hits the pitch and rolls to long-on's left

balls.description: Siddharth to Nitish Kumar, FOUR runs
balls.commentary: Reddy is getting into overs with boundaries. Dances down and goes inside out over extra cover this time. Skimmer. Got to the pitch nicely and the ball was a couple of stumps away from him, well in reach
Wide, low full toss, slapped to deep cover
Looks like a drop
Length ball is worked to long-on
WIde yorker darted, right on the line. Let go
WIde and full, cross-bat caress takes it to deep cover

balls.description: Avesh Khan to Klaasen, FOUR runs
balls.commentary: Freebie. Short, wide and sits up. Klaasen lifts the back leg and slaps it from shoulder-height over cover

balls.description: Avesh Khan to Klaasen, FOUR runs
balls.commentary: Beats cover and the infielder is beaten in a long chase. Remarkable timing. Back of length, top of off, Klaasen stood just outside the stumps and was able to punch it with a vertical bat
Short outside off, dragged to deep midwicket with a pull

balls.description: Avesh Khan to Klaasen, FOUR runs
balls.commentary: Smeared into the ground and beats cover again, no one chasing this time. Pitched up wide, Klaasen stands his ground, bends his knees and gets his hands low before blazing it through

balls.description: Avesh Khan to Klaasen, FOUR runs
balls.commentary: Pulls and gets another boundary! Avesh's bouncers not working. This one was pace-on but didn't zip off the pitch, Klaasen had to wait a while before pulling from the front of the body through midwicket
Length around off, 130ks, driven on top of the bounce to long-off
Gets away with a wide one. Front-of-the-hand length ball is cut to deep cover point

balls.description: Rathi to Nitish Kumar,  SIX runs
balls.commentary: Slot ball has been thumped! Legbreak on middle allows Reddy the step hit. Bends his back knee and lifts it over long-on. Sumptuous lofted drive

balls.description: Rathi to Nitish Kumar,  SIX runs
balls.commentary: Flat six over long-off! Too full again, in Reddy's reach. Doesn't have to do much before swiping across. 100 up for SRH, that didn't seem possible a while back
Back of length, pulled to deep midwicket
Wide and shortish, cut to deep point

Goes for the reverse sweep and misses. Given wide, LSG review. Back of length lands outside off and turns away sharply. No part of the ball is inside the imaginary line over the tramline. LSG burn the review

balls.description: Rathi to Nitish Kumar,  SIX runs
balls.commentary: Reddy's picking them and punishing them! Front of the hand this time. On a length outside off, no turn. Reddy doesn't move, swings across and clears long-on with ease

balls.description: Prince Yadav to Klaasen,  SIX runs
balls.commentary: No second invitation needed. Klaasen gets the slot ball on middle, stays leg side of the ball and muscles a drive over the bowler. 80 metres
Short just outside off, pulled into the ground. Goes to long-on

balls.description: Prince Yadav to Nitish Kumar, no run
balls.commentary: Hit hard but not timed, finds cover. Reddy trying to lift a length ball but gets it highish off the bat and into the ground

balls.description: Prince Yadav to Nitish Kumar,  1 run
balls.commentary: Nails the yorker. That option will be taken a lot from now on. At the stumps, jammed to long-on

balls.description: Prince Yadav to Klaasen, no run
balls.commentary: Another yorker. On middle and off again, Klaasen jams it back to the bowler again
Good change up. Slow bouncer just outside off, pulled to deep square leg. Flags fly and cheers return as Klaasen brings up fifty. Timeout
Yorker on leg, flicked to deep square leg. 132ks

balls.description: Avesh Khan to Nitish Kumar,  1 run
balls.commentary: Follows through with the off drive with panache, hits long-off and gets fifty. Razor-sharp celebration, scissoring the bat under the chin
Very low full toss on off, springs down a touch and drives to deep point
Short just outside off, cut to deep cover

balls.description: Avesh Khan to Nitish Kumar, no run
balls.commentary: Yorker is chomped back to the bowler. Just outside off, Reddy looking to smother it with a full follow-through. Can't. Good bowling

balls.description: Avesh Khan to Nitish Kumar,  SIX runs
balls.commentary: No changes or improvisation needed to whack away a yorker. Gets forward, then low and lifts a cover drive. Getting under that ball was the main thing
Can't miss lengths, doesn't miss by much. Near yorker lands just in front of the crease, Klaasen clears his front leg and drives straight over the bowler
And the full toss doesn't travel... A bit wide, slapped wide of long-off
This full toss.... almost does the trick. Shin-high and well wide, slapped on the bounce to deep cover, running in
The wide line works. Reddy went for the right shot, lifting it off the front foot like he has against pace and spin. Can't get the height on it, deep cover takes it
balls.dismissal: Nitish Kumar Reddy c Prince Yadav b Siddharth 56 (33b 3x4 5x6 55m) SR: 169.69

balls.commentary: Goes for the wide yorker again, the full toss goes outside the tramline
Yorker on off, driven straight to long-off. Siddharth trusting his lengths

This is a good ball. Klaasen was premeditating the sweep while standing still, so Siddharth goes wide with the low full toss. Hands reaching out means ball has to be hit into the ground, goes to deep midwicket. Good over from Siddharth

Premeditation ends up with a diving catch for Pant. Klaasen shaped to play the reverse lap before the delivery. Avesh went full around off, then it became a matter of execution. But Klaasen lobbed it to Pant's right
balls.dismissal: Heinrich Klaasen c †Pant b Avesh Khan 62 (41b 5x4 2x6 75m) SR: 151.21

balls.commentary: Chopped on and the stadium in quiet. Avesh goes back of length outside off, cross seamer, Dubey dances down and looks to cut it. Not enough room, swung too high too. Under-edge goes back onto hot stump.
balls.dismissal: Harsh Dubey b Avesh Khan 0 (1b 0x4 0x6 1m) SR: 0

balls.description: Avesh Khan to Shivang, no run
balls.commentary: Length on off, Shivang wanted to tap and run. Defended into the pitch, back onto his face
Full on leg, flicked to long-on

balls.description: Avesh Khan to Verma, no run
balls.commentary: Goes outside off, yorker follows him and it's punched to cover.
Will farm strike, Aniket did go for the boundary. It was a full toss after all. Swiped across, got it along the ground to deep midwicket

balls.description: Prince Yadav to Verma, 1 leg bye
balls.commentary: Nutmegged, Pant saves three... Dives to the left. Aniket takes the run. Was a yorker on leg, cleared the front leg and went for the flick. It clipped the back leg

balls.description: Prince Yadav to Shivang, FOUR runs
balls.commentary: Why farm strike when Shivang can do the job? He crawls outside off, stays in the crease and laps a very low full toss on off, over short fine leg
Yorkers are so back in style! 137.4ks, slanting in and hitting offf stump. Shivang got low and looked to hit straight, ball was too low and too quick
balls.dismissal: Shivang Kumar b Prince Yadav 5 (4b 1x4 0x6 6m) SR: 125

balls.description: Prince Yadav to Harshal, FOUR runs
balls.commentary: Bowlers getting the boundaries for SRH. His knew it'd be a yorker attempt. Ended up being a full ball on off, he swung straight, opened the face a touch to lift the ball over extra cover
Bouncer beats the pull, Pant doesn't collect clearly and they SRH review for a wide. Side-on replay shows ball just under the helmet. No measurements needed
Swing and a miss, Pant throws down the striker's stumps. Harshal well short. Was a short slow ball outside off, Aniket went for the pull and took off right after being beaten
